name: Lab Interpretation — Simple Anemia Panel
description: Level 1 — MIMIC hadm 22585261 — single abnormality pattern, macrocytic posthemorrhagic anemia
prompt:
Interpret the following laboratory results for a 44-year-old male admitted with hematemesis and melena:

CBC:
- Hemoglobin: 8.3 g/dL (reference: 14.0–18.0)
- Hematocrit: 25% (reference: 40–52%)
- Red Blood Cells: 2.52 million/uL (reference: 4.6–6.2)
- MCH: 37.3 pg (reference: 27–32) — ABNORMAL HIGH
- MCV: 103 fL (reference: 80–100) — ABNORMAL HIGH
- INR: 1.3 (reference: 0.9–1.1)

Coagulation: PT 13.6 sec (reference: 9.4–12.5)
Chemistry: BUN 21 mg/dL (reference: 6–20), Albumin 3.3 g/dL (reference: 3.5–5.2)

Provide clinical interpretation of these results.

abnormal_values.significance: Severe anemia consistent with significant hemorrhage
Markedly reduced erythrocyte mass from acute blood loss
Macrocytic pattern suggesting possible nutritional deficiency (B12/folate)
Mild coagulopathy, consistent with hepatic or nutritional compromise
Elevated BUN-to-creatinine ratio in upper GI bleed (protein load from digested blood)

expected.interpretation: Severe normocytic-to-macrocytic anemia with elevated BUN in context of upper GI hemorrhage. Pattern consistent with acute posthemorrhagic anemia superimposed on possible nutritional deficiency (macrocytosis from B12/folate, treated with supplementation).
expected.pattern_recognition: Upper GI bleed pattern: severe anemia (Hgb 8.3) + elevated BUN (21) from digested blood protein reabsorption + macrocytosis suggesting underlying nutritional deficiency.
differential_considerations: Underlying vitamin B12 or folate deficiency (causing macrocytosis)
Mild coagulopathy from hepatic disease or nutritional deficiency
